task.summary: Evaluate the multi-agent and external-model planning output for static Radeq /ukazky pages: chatbot/pruvodce, automation, and offer/e-shop examples with security and data-protection constraints.

model_or_worker.name: Codex supervisor, Codex GPT agents, Claude CLI, Gemini CLI, and Decision Mesh

reasoning_profile.change_policy: Use as an implementation plan only after owner approves the recorded gates; product code changes remain blocked until approval.

token_efficiency_profile.context_scope: Repo file map, targeted product files/tests, prior agent summaries, redacted external-model prompt packets, and Radeq project mesh packet.

input_packet_summary.summary: A bounded Radeq planning packet covering static Astro/GitHub Pages constraints, first-wave /ukazky routes, no-model chatbot boundaries, prior advisory findings, and owner stop conditions.
allowed_context: repo file map and test scripts
redacted Claude and Gemini advisory prompts
forbidden_context: raw model output as source of truth

output_pointer.summary: Accepted planning synthesis: implement /ukazky as Czech static Astro pages with typed data, a local rule-based chatbot island, explicit security copy, sitemap/test coverage, and owner gates for navigation, language scope, email/provider work, and demo-route migration.

prompt_or_input_delta.summary: The prompt packet was narrowed after owner clarified that the chatbot should remain static/rule-based and that AI should be framed only as an adjacent possibility.
prompt_or_input_delta.rollback_path: Remove this eval and the related plan/work-log entries if owner rejects the static /ukazky implementation direction.

verification_evidence.evidence: Product repo file map, package scripts, Astro routes, BaseLayout, sitemap, and existing Playwright/Vitest tests were inspected read-only before planning.

Radeq project mesh packet identified static public site, offer positioning, SEO, lead capture, runtime observability, and 3D mascot constraints; plan includes relevant owner gates and stop conditions.

Four Codex GPT agents produced independent read-only plans covering IA, chatbot architecture, security/privacy, and QA; outputs converged on static pages, no-model chatbot, explicit handoff, and tests.
Claude CLI and Gemini CLI were invoked with redacted advisory prompts; Gemini capacity limitations were recorded and lower-confidence generic output was not treated as source of truth.
